name: Todoist
description: The task manager that bridges personal and professional. 50M+ users. Natural language task entry, projects, subtasks, labels, calendar view, team collaboration.

pricing.pro: $4/mo (annual) or $5/mo — 300 projects, calendar layout, custom reminders, unlimited activity history
pricing.business: $6/user/mo (annual) — shared workspaces, team projects, roles & permissions

web.headline: Clarity, finally.
web.subheadline: Join 50+ million professionals who simplify work and life with the world's #1 to-do list app.

top_praise: Organizational power — multi-year users credit it for transforming productivity
Flexibility & customization — projects, labels, sections, folders
Voice input (Ramble) for hands-free task capture
top_complaints: Data loss during forced updates — most severe recurring issue
UI changes & navigation — redesign hides common features like inbox
Broken functionality — notifications, widgets, swipe gestures fail post-update
Subscription friction — legacy vs new tiers confuse users; paywalled reminders
AI feature bloat — users want bug fixes, not new AI features
one_star_issues: Complete task deletion after forced update
Years of data gone with no restore option
web_themes: Price hike of 25-40% in Dec 2025 driving users to TickTick and others
5-project cap on free tier frustrates former power users
sentiment.positioning: Market leader in personal task management. GTD-focused power users. Strong brand trust but recent update debacles and price hike creating vulnerability.
